mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=4.64 as of 2024-11-01 vintage, down from cycle peak of 5.33
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS-declining periods are not significantly higher than the unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like threshold), it signals an emerging recession that equity markets have not yet fully priced, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=4.2% as of 2024-11-01, up from cycle low of 3.4%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the effect disappears when tested on post-2015 data excluding the 2020 shock.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL determines the real policy rate; when nominal FEDFUNDS exceeds trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation by more than 2 percentage points, real rates are restrictive and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. Neither series alone captures this restrictiveness.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate > 2pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=4.64% vs CPIAUCSL YoY inflation near 2.7% implying positive real rate near 2pp
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: Hit rate of negative 20-day returns under the joint trigger falls below 50%, or Rank IC computed only on the interaction term is not statistically distinguishable from Rank IC of FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Rising HOUST (housing starts) signals forward-looking consumer confidence and construction-driven demand, but this positive relationship to S&P 500 forward returns only holds when FEDFUNDS is falling or low, because in a tightening regime rising starts instead foreshadow overheating and policy risk that reverses the sign.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases >5% over 3 months AND FEDFUNDS is flat or declining
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=1352k revised as of 2024-10-01 vintage amid FEDFUNDS at 4.64% post cuts
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with recovering housing sector
failure_condition: The positive HOUST-return relationship fails to reverse sign when tested against a tightening-regime subsample (FEDFUNDS rising >0.5pp over 6 months), or overall Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in INDPRO reflects improving industrial output and corporate earnings momentum, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as markets incorporate incrementally stronger production data.
failure_condition: 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO month-over-month change is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) across a 5-year rolling window, or sign flips persist for more than 2 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS retail sales growth decelerates sharply, falling below its trailing 6-month average growth rate by more than 1 standard deviation, it signals weakening consumer demand that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth falls more than 1 std dev below its trailing 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value=708290 million as of 2024-10-01 vintage
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following the trigger are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is not robust when excluding 2008-2009 and 2020 crisis periods.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of decelerating PAYEMS growth alongside a rising UNRATE indicates a genuine labor-market turning point rather than noisy monthly volatility, jointly signaling recession risk that neither series alone reliably captures, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND UNRATE rising for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value=158079k and UNRATE=4.2% as of 2024-11-01 vintage
regime_hint: labor market cooling into potential recession
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns under the joint trigger are not significantly more negative than under either single-series trigger alone, or Rank IC of the interaction term is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPIAUCSL print that surprises above the recent 6-month trend by more than 0.2 index points signals renewed inflation risk, prompting immediate equity sell-offs over the next trading week as markets reprice expected Fed tightening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM change exceeds trailing 6-month average MoM change by more than 0.2 index points
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following upside CPI surprises are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears once FEDFUNDS is below 2% (near zero lower bound periods).
